qa_pairs.question: How does one initiate a graphical Abaqus session using JupyterLab?
qa_pairs.answer: To initiate a graphical Abaqus session using JupyterLab, first access a JupyterHub desktop session via one of the provided links (e.g., FIR, NARVAL, RORQUAL). In the "Available Module" section on the left, highlight an Abaqus module such as `abaqus/2026`, and then click "Load." An "Abaqus (VNC)" icon will appear on the desktop, which, when clicked, will automatically start Abaqus in a remote Jupyter desktop.

qa_pairs.question: Is the VncViewer client a recommended tool for graphical use of Abaqus on Alliance clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: No, using the VncViewer client for graphical Abaqus sessions is considered an obsolete method. It is recommended that users opt for OpenOnDemand or JupyterLab instead.
